questions: Identify the major digital and print publication produced as a joint project by the IUSSP and UNFPA that serves as the direct lineage successor to the 'UN Manual X: Indirect Techniques for Demographic Estimation,' designed to provide updated tools for analyzing limited or defective data. Once identified, provide the full 'Suggested citation' for the specific section within this work authored by T.A. Moultrie titled 'Evaluation and correction of fertility data,' ensuring you include the full list of editors and the publisher.

It requires Wide information aggregation to construct the complete citation for a specific section, necessitating the retrieval of the author, year, full list of editors, and publisher details found in the target source.

rounds.answer_a:
The joint publication is "Tools for Demographic Estimation." The suggested citation for the chapter by T.A. Moultrie is: Moultrie, T.A. 2013. "Evaluation and correction of fertility data." In *Tools for Demographic Estimation*, edited by T. Moultrie, R.E. Dorrington, A.G. Hill, K. Hill, I.M. Timæus, and B. Zaba. Paris: International Union for the Scientific Study of Population. [1]

## References
[1] Moultrie, T.A. 2013. "Evaluation and correction of fertility data." In *Tools for Demographic Estimation*, edited by T. Moultrie, R.E. Dorrington, A.G. Hill, K. Hill, I.M. Timæus, and B. Zaba. Paris: International Union for the Scientific Study of Population. https://demographicestimation.iussp.org/content/evaluation-and-correction-fertility-data
rounds.answer_b:
The major joint IUSSP-UNFPA digital and print publication succeeding UN Manual X is "Indirect Demographic Estimation: Methods for Applications in Developing Countries"[1]. It updates tools for analyzing limited or defective data, with T.A. Moultrie’s "Evaluation and correction of fertility data" serving as a critical, specialized section that advances the manual’s legacy for developing country contexts[1].

## References
[1] Moultrie, T.A. 2001. Evaluation and correction of fertility data. In: Preston, S.H., Heuveline, P., Guillot, M. (eds.) Indirect Demographic Estimation: Methods for Applications in Developing Countries. International Union for the Scientific Study of Population (IUSSP) and United Nations Population Fund (UNFPA).
rounds.judge_reasoning: Agent A correctly identified the publication as 'Tools for Demographic Estimation' and provided the accurate citation with the correct list of editors (Moultrie, Dorrington, Hill, Hill, Timæus, and Zaba). Agent B hallucinated the book title and the editors, attributing the work to Preston, Heuveline, and Guillot (who are authors of a different demography textbook, not this specific IUSSP/UNFPA manual). Agent B failed the fundamental logic of the query.
